source.title: British Society of Gastroenterology guidelines on sedation in gastrointestinal endoscopy
source.population: People undergoing gastrointestinal endoscopy with recommendation-specific sedation, procedure, age, and comorbidity contexts.
source.setting: Endoscopy pre-assessment, procedure, recovery, and discharge.
source.exclusions: Exact only for selected procedure-context, monitored-recovery, discharge-assessment, follow-up-planning, and safety-instruction documentation. It does not support retrospective diagnosis, pathology interpretation, treatment, or universal post-endoscopy follow-up.
source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports documenting recovery progress, baseline consciousness or function, vital monitoring, complications during recovery, information exchange, follow-up planning, and discharge advice.

items.final_wording: Supports documented oxygenation, ventilation, circulation, consciousness, temperature, pain or other recovery observations, and use of a recovery score where appropriate.

source.title: Standards for Postanesthesia Care
source.population: Patients who receive general anesthesia, regional anesthesia, or monitored anesthesia care.
source.setting: PACU or equivalent postanesthesia recovery location.
source.exclusions: Exact for transfer, arrival evaluation, handover, recovery monitoring and records, discharge criteria, and qualified discharge responsibility. It does not generate discharge or treatment decisions.

exact_location.heading: Standard III — continual evaluation and recovery record

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports documented oxygenation, ventilation, circulation, consciousness, temperature, pain or other recovery observations, and use of a recovery score where appropriate.

items.rationale: The ASA standard supports documenting recovery observations after monitored anesthesia care without generating a recovery conclusion or treatment.

items.final_wording: Supports documenting recovery progress, baseline consciousness or function, vital monitoring, complications during recovery, information exchange, follow-up planning, and discharge advice.

source.title: British Society of Gastroenterology guidelines on sedation in gastrointestinal endoscopy
source.population: People undergoing gastrointestinal endoscopy with recommendation-specific sedation, procedure, age, and comorbidity contexts.
source.setting: Endoscopy pre-assessment, procedure, recovery, and discharge.
source.exclusions: Exact only for selected procedure-context, monitored-recovery, discharge-assessment, follow-up-planning, and safety-instruction documentation. It does not support retrospective diagnosis, pathology interpretation, treatment, or universal post-endoscopy follow-up.
source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports documenting recovery progress, baseline consciousness or function, vital monitoring, complications during recovery, information exchange, follow-up planning, and discharge advice.

items.rationale: The exact BSG section supports recovery progress, complications, information exchange, and follow-up planning only for gastrointestinal endoscopy sedation.

items.final_wording: Supports formal documented discharge assessment, recovery from sedation, absence of complications, vital and respiratory status, pain or discomfort review, procedure-appropriate instructions, contact information, and activity restrictions after sedation.

source.title: British Society of Gastroenterology guidelines on sedation in gastrointestinal endoscopy
source.population: People undergoing gastrointestinal endoscopy with recommendation-specific sedation, procedure, age, and comorbidity contexts.
source.setting: Endoscopy pre-assessment, procedure, recovery, and discharge.
source.exclusions: Exact only for selected procedure-context, monitored-recovery, discharge-assessment, follow-up-planning, and safety-instruction documentation. It does not support retrospective diagnosis, pathology interpretation, treatment, or universal post-endoscopy follow-up.

exact_location.heading: Recommendations 18–19 — suitability for discharge and instructions

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports formal documented discharge assessment, recovery from sedation, absence of complications, vital and respiratory status, pain or discomfort review, procedure-appropriate instructions, contact information, and activity restrictions after sedation.

items.rationale: The discharge section was reviewed but not mapped to a generic post-sedation workflow because it is procedure- and endoscopy-specific.
